title: surf
author: Site Manager

riskAssessment.controls: Secure hoarding, signage, marshals, controlled gates, monitored exclusion zones
Approved traffic management plan, temporary barriers, speed controls, competent TM operatives
Lift plan, appointed person, LOLER compliant equipment, pre-use checks, exclusion zone
Permit system, lock-off/tag-out, verification testing, authorised persons only
Design checks, TW coordinator approval, staged loading, routine inspections
Monitoring, low-vibration methods, restricted hours, hearing protection

riskAssessment.hazard: Traffic interface during live carriageway works
riskAssessment.controls: Approved TM plan, lane closures, advance signing and lookout
Water suppression, guarded equipment, PPE and exclusion zones
Thermal PPE, controlled work zones and burn response kits

riskAssessment.hazard: Poor compaction due to temperature loss
riskAssessment.controls: Delivery timing, rolling pattern and continuous temperature checks

riskAssessment.hazard: Traffic interface at final surfacing stage
riskAssessment.controls: Strict closures, marshals and controlled reopening criteria

riskAssessment.hazard: Inadequate skid resistance due to workmanship
riskAssessment.controls: Approved mix, laying temperature checks and QA sign-off

riskAssessment.hazard: Manual handling injuries when moving covers/frames
riskAssessment.controls: Mechanical lifting keys and team lifts where required
Immediate guarding, barrier controls and supervised access

riskAssessment.hazard: Vehicle strike during short-duration lane closures
riskAssessment.controls: Mobile lane closure setup, lookout and compliant signing
Heat-resistant PPE and controlled application zones
Approved TM plan, advance signing, taper setup and competent operatives
Safe pedestrian diversions, marshals and clear information signage
Heat PPE, controlled discharge zones, no standing behind paver screed, burn kits available
Segregation, banksman, reverse alarms/cameras, one-way plan
Approved traffic management, lane closures, barriers, lookout/marshal
Ventilation/working position controls, RPE where assessed, exposure minimisation
Lifting aids, team lift, training and task rotation

riskAssessment.hazard: Weather impact on compaction quality and safety
riskAssessment.controls: Weather checks, revised laying plan, stop-work criteria for rain/low temperature

methodStatementSteps: Review IFC drawings, utility constraints, temporary works and ITP hold points.
Deliver site induction and activity brief with competency verification.
Implement traffic and pedestrian management controls before workface opening.
Set out works, verify levels and tolerances, complete permit checks.
Execute works in approved sequence with quality checkpoints and sign-offs.
Complete testing/commissioning, snag close-out and as-built record updates.
Implement traffic management and confirm milling extents.
Plane carriageway to specified depth and profile.
Sweep and inspect milled surface for defects.
Dispose/recycle arisings in accordance with waste controls.
Prepare substrate and apply tack coat.
Lay binder course to specified thickness and crossfall.
Compact to density requirements using agreed rolling sequence.
Record quality checks and release for surface course.
Verify binder readiness and weather window.
Lay surface course with controlled joints and finish.
Complete rolling sequence and texture compliance checks.
Open to traffic only after release criteria are met.
Set local traffic management and isolate work area.
Remove and prepare existing frame and surrounding surfacing.
Raise/reset frame to finished level and reinstate surround.
Verify level tolerance and reopen on cure completion.
Set traffic management and clean application surfaces.
Apply road markings to layout drawings and standards.
Install studs/signage/furniture with compliant fixings.
Inspect visibility, alignment and retroreflectivity where required.
Brief team on approved traffic management layout and sequence.
Deploy signs, cones and barriers from safe zones.
Monitor traffic flow and maintain controls throughout shift.
Dismantle traffic management in reverse safe sequence.
Confirm approved mix design, delivery plan, weather window and target layer thickness.
Implement traffic management and workface segregation before surfacing starts.
Prepare substrate (clean, dry, to level) and apply tack coat to specification.
Lay asphalt using paver in controlled passes with joint management plan.
Roll and compact to target density/voids with documented rolling pattern.
Carry out temperature, level and compaction quality checks and record results.
Reinstate road markings/signage and reopen area only after release criteria met.
emergencyProcedures: Stop works and make area safe
Use project-specific rescue and first aid response
Stop traffic interface works and escalate incidents
Stop operations and secure live lane interface
Call emergency services for significant incidents
Stop roadworks and protect incident scene
Contact emergency services and traffic authority
Cool burns with clean water and seek urgent first aid
Escalate major incident via emergency chain
Secure traffic interface and prevent secondary incidents
environmentalControls: Noise control and restricted working windows
Waste asphalt recycling and transfer documentation
